channel.title: X407 Insights
channel.description: Insights on AI-to-AI payments, machine commerce, API monetization, receipts, and enterprise trust.

item.title: AI-to-AI Payments vs. API Keys: What Actually Changes
item.description: Why payment challenges, proofs, and receipts outperform static credentials for agent-native monetization.

item.title: Enterprise Trust Starts with Receipts, Auditability, and Evidence
item.description: Why receipts and evidence bundles change enterprise procurement and adoption velocity.

item.title: Why Agent Commerce Infrastructure Becomes the Next Platform Layer
item.description: Why monetization, trust, receipts, routing, and packaging converge into the next platform layer.

item.title: What Is AI-to-AI Payment Infrastructure?
item.description: A clear explanation of the infrastructure layer behind programmable AI-to-AI payments, receipts, and monetized machine commerce.

item.title: How to Monetize APIs for AI Agents Without API Keys
item.description: A provider-oriented guide to monetized API access using programmable payment challenges and receipts instead of static credentials.

item.title: Why HTTP 402 Becomes Strategic for Paid APIs
item.description: Why HTTP 402 matters when providers need programmable terms, route-level monetization, and evidence-backed paid API access.

item.title: AWS, Layer 1, and Machine Commerce Infrastructure
item.description: How X407 combines AWS-hosted application infrastructure, built-in control systems, and Layer 1 settlement orchestration for machine commerce.

item.title: X407 vs Legacy Billing vs Chain-Only Rails
item.description: A commercial comparison page across monetization, enterprise trust, AWS application infrastructure, and Layer 1 settlement orchestration.

item.title: X407 Pilot Program
item.description: A pilot launch path for enterprise agent payment infrastructure, monetized APIs, and machine commerce systems.

item.title: Agentic Funding, Transaction Orchestration, and Explorer
item.description: A high-level system page for funding agents, governing transactions, and tracking activity through one explorer.
